description: Add document_start injection support for plugins that need to cache volatile auth state (like localStorage tokens) before the host app's JavaScript deletes it. Discord and similar apps clear localStorage after reading the token, making the current document_complete injection too late.

userStories.title: Support an optional earlyInject script for plugins that runs at document_start
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want to specify an optional early-inject script that runs at document_start (before page JavaScript) so that I can cache volatile auth state like localStorage tokens before the host app deletes them
acceptanceCriteria: Plugins can define an optional `earlyInject` field in their package.json opentabs config that points to a small JS file
The early-inject script runs in the page MAIN world at document_start timing, before any page scripts execute
The early-inject script has access to localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, and document (but NOT document.body)
The early-inject script can write to globalThis to pass data to the main adapter IIFE
The main adapter IIFE injection at document_complete is unchanged — it reads from globalThis to get cached values
Plugins without earlyInject work exactly as before (no change to existing behavior)
The early-inject script is bundled by opentabs-plugin build alongside the adapter IIFE
The extension's manifest.json registers the early-inject scripts via content_scripts with run_at: document_start, OR the background script injects them on tabs.onUpdated with status: loading
CSP is not a concern (file-based injection bypasses page CSP)
